description_long: This is a terracotta vase with a bulbous lower body that tapers into a long, narrow neck and a slightly flared rim. The surface is predominantly a reddish-brown color, with visible wear and some surface abrasions, suggesting age or frequent handling. Decorative geometric bands encircle the shoulder of the vase, adding visual interest and texture. The mouth of the vase appears slightly chipped, and there is a small hole on the side of the body, possibly from damage or as part of its original design. The overall form is elegant and functional, suitable for holding flowers or liquids. Its rustic finish and simple ornamentation make it well-suited for display in outdoor or urban public spaces.
description: A reddish-brown terracotta vase with a bulbous body, long neck, geometric bands, and visible wear, including a small side hole and chipped rim.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the rounded body and the narrow neck extending away, with the mouth of the vase not visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bulbous body, long neck, geometric banding, and the small hole on the side.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view focusing on the narrow neck and flared rim, with the opening and some interior visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full profile of the vase, including the geometric bands and the small side hole.
